206860.explanation: This is why council members are elected, and LPF believes they should always be the ones accountable.

206861.explanation: This is why council members are elected, and LPF believes they should always be the ones accountable.

206862.explanation: This is why council members are elected, and LPF believes they should always be the ones accountable.

206863.explanation: This is a misleading proposition. Every municipality has its own financial policy.

206864.explanation: This wording is too black-and-white. What about a travel agency that plans to sell holiday flights?

206865.explanation: This is a waste of money, as Eindhoven has too few locations where new businesses can establish themselves.

206866.explanation: This is flogging a dead horse.

206867.explanation: Collection costs are too high, while enforcement leaves much to be desired.

206868.explanation: Keeping the city liveable will require clear agreements on exactly when certain businesses are allowed to open.

206869.explanation: This is pure interference with free enterprise.

206870.explanation: This will open the floodgates, making liveability and safety in the city nigh impossible to monitor.

206871.explanation: As a municipality, we cannot disadvantage our own residents. If we do, it will do severe damage to the acceptance of genuine refugees.

206872.explanation: No, we do not need more money, what we need is clear agreements with them about what Dutch society can expect from them.

206873.explanation: Absolutely not! Eindhoven, in cooperation with other municipalities via the Association of Netherlands Municipalities (VNG), needs to convince the national government to stop the flow of asylum seekers.

206874.explanation: Absolute nonsense. You cannot hold the modern generation responsible for something that was abolished over 150 years ago.

206875.explanation: People who work eight hours a day have 16 hours to do this at home. Separation of church and state.

206876.explanation: Indeed. There is already a major housing shortage, and the workers will also need additional training.

206877.explanation: But let us first see where there is space for these businesses to set up in our city and region.

206878.explanation: Implementation should remain in the hands of the municipality.

206879.explanation: The government should stay out of how people practise their hobbies.

206880.explanation: If the municipality wants to safeguard liveability and security in the city, there will need to be discussions on what is and is not permitted.

206881.explanation: If this means there will be less bureaucracy, then YES! Liveability and safety should not be affected, however.

206882.explanation: This is what banks are for. Municipalities could offer advice, however.

206883.explanation: Provided no irreparable damage is done to the monument.

206884.explanation: Our scarce land is too valuable for this.

206885.explanation: This already happens via extra allowances.

206886.explanation: Provided it is financially feasible, otherwise they will close again after a year due to lack of funding.

206887.explanation: Not for everybody! Libraries are also expensive to run. Making things ‘free’ for everyone in the city means they simply pay for it in other ways. Membership should still remain affordable for people on low incomes, however, and we support the policy that current students and young people aged under 21 should not have to pay.

206888.explanation: This will not work in practice, unless we are talking about separate sports halls.

206890.explanation: This is a good thing, as it will reduce the distance between local residents and enforcement officers.

206891.explanation: This would be ideal. The problem will be a lack of manpower and funding.

206892.explanation: A uniform exudes authority. No other head covering is appropriate.

206893.explanation: BOAs need to be able to defend themselves against violent citizens. Something is better than nothing.

206894.explanation: Maintaining safety and liveability will mean more security cameras.

206896.explanation: This is what banks are for. The municipality could, as it has done before, introduce a specific guarantee or subsidy scheme.

206897.explanation: If we want more family homes, this could be a nice solution under the right conditions.

206898.explanation: Not only smaller homes, but also affordable for those on a budget.

206900.explanation: The target group should, of course, be well-defined.

206901.explanation: If this means that the surface area of the city will increase, then with due care this is acceptable. But no impact on city parks.

206903.explanation: Where this is necessary, yes always. It is socially irresponsible to allow this fraud to continue.

206904.explanation: Yes, provided it was not the debtor’s fault. But there must be targets and limitations. If someone is not a repeat offender, a settlement can help get them back on track. The municipality can also work with creditors to reduce the debt.

206908.explanation: Because many parking spaces are set to disappear in Eindhoven, we will need to ensure additional spots for people with limited mobility.

206910.explanation: Low-traffic means fewer cars, but the city must still remain accessible, with enough parking spaces.

206911.explanation: This will throw the city’s infrastructure into disarray, and Eindhoven does not have the space besides.

206912.explanation: Traffic flow at 70 km/h is better than at 50 km/h.

206913.explanation: No, not more anti-car policy in our city.

206914.explanation: No, this is unnecessary age discrimination.

206915.explanation: We need to ensure that these volunteers can continue to do their work.

206856.explanation: A healthy business climate with room for new tech companies generates innovation, jobs and economic growth in the region. By investing in enterprise and less regulatory red tape, Eindhoven will remain attractive to companies and investments.

206857.explanation: ASML is important to the region and can grow itself, without any preferential treatment to the detriment of local businesses. Tax revenue must never be used for the forced sale of land. If expansion is necessary, ASML will purchase the land itself, not taxpayers.

206858.explanation: Small modular reactors generate reliable and affordable energy, unlike unprofitable wind turbines and solar farms. Nuclear energy will make the energy supply more stable, reducing costs in the long run.

206859.explanation: Seniors deserve a genuine say, not an extra advisory council. Input should take place via referendums and direct engagement, so that everybody is heard equally and policy does not become mired in yet another discussion group.

206860.explanation: A citizens’ assembly gives influence to only a small, randomly selected group, and does not give power to all residents. We therefore support referendums, so that everybody can vote and decisions are genuinely taken by the population.

206861.explanation: Important decisions about Eindhoven affect everybody, and should therefore be taken by the residents themselves. A referendum gives residents direct influence over decisions that shape their city and neighbourhood.

206862.explanation: Residents know best what their neighbourhoods need, not the city hall. A fixed neighbourhood budget will give Eindhoven residents a genuine say, and direct funds to where they will make a real local difference.

206863.explanation: The OZB must not be a municipal cash cow, especially not in times where costs are rising. Limiting hikes will keep housing affordable, and prevent residents from being faced with unexpected cost increases.

206864.explanation: This will chase companies away, costing jobs and damaging the local economy. Enterprise should remain free, without ideological hurdles or additional municipal bureaucracy.

206865.explanation: Eindhoven will become appealing to companies through low costs, fewer regulations, and rapid permit allocation, not expensive advertising campaigns. The focus should lie on eliminating bureaucracy and giving freedom to enterprise, so that businesses will want to come here themselves.

206866.explanation: Eindhoven became great through enterprise and innovation, not regulations and bureaucracy. Bringing the head office back will create jobs, attract investment, and show that Eindhoven is once again supporting a strong and open business climate.

206867.explanation: Dog ownership should not be a cash cow for the municipality, and is an unfair additional cost for dog owners. It would be better for that money to stay with the residents themselves, rather than disappearing in to the city’s coffers.

206868.explanation: Businesspeople themselves know best when to serve their customers. Fewer rules and more freedom will create more turnover, employment, and improve the business climate.

206869.explanation: A ban on ‘non-sustainable’ advertising is government interference, and impinges on the freedom of our businesses. Residents should be able to decide for themselves what they buy and consume, without the municipality pushing them in a certain direction through bans.

206870.explanation: Rather than solving the problem, distribution across the city will only create more crowding and pressure on neighbourhoods. The focus should be on shutting down the asylum-seeker centres in Eindhoven completely.

206871.explanation: Rental social housing is scarce, and should be reserved first and foremost for our own residents. Priority for recognised refugees puts the housing market under additional strain and is unfair to our own starters, families and seniors.

206872.explanation: Integration is first and foremost the responsibility of newcomers themselves, not the taxpayers. Municipal funds should go to essential services for all Eindhoven residents, not to more and more integration projects without any clear results.

206873.explanation: More asylum seekers create additional pressure on safety, housing, and facilities, while Eindhoven is already struggling in these respects. The municipality needs to stop accepting asylum seekers, and opt for stability, safety and space for our own residents.

206874.explanation: Eindhoven needs to look ahead and address the real issues, instead of taking part in performative guilt politics.

206875.explanation: Public buildings are for everybody and should remain neutral, without religious provisions that not everybody will use. Faith is a personal matter and belongs in the home and in churches, mosques or synagogues, not town halls or libraries.

206876.explanation: Companies are welcome and ensure jobs and growth, but labour migration should not put additional pressure on housing, public services, or liveability. We should therefore always consider the consequences, not simply shut up shop or throw open the floodgates.

206877.explanation: Forced relocation of companies will chase jobs and businesses out of the city, weakening Eindhoven’s economy. The municipality needs to stop its ideological environmental policy and concentrate on employment, growth, and a robust local economy.

206878.explanation: More local vegetation makes streets more attractive, cooler, and a more pleasant place to live in. If residents wish to get to work themselves, the municipality should support them.

206879.explanation: Recreational fishing is an innocent hobby enjoyed by many Eindhoven residents, one that has a place within our public spaces. A ban is excessive, limits freedoms, and comes down too harshly on ordinary residents.

206880.explanation: Greater freedom for the hospitality industry will create a lively city and a stronger local economy. Effective enforcement will maintain safety, while residents and visitors can continue to enjoy the city’s nightlife.

206881.explanation: Fewer regulations and faster permits will allow for more life in the city and greater freedom for local initiatives. Festivals and parties bring residents together, strengthen local culture, and help businesses, clubs and societies move forward.

206882.explanation: Municipal funds belong to residents and should go to essential services, not high-risk loans. Sustainability is up to residents themselves, taxpayers should not be the ones to foot the bill.

206883.explanation: Protection of monuments demands care and specialist expertise, not more or fewer regulations. Sustainability measures, where appropriate, can be voluntary and implemented without compromising the character of the property or imposing extra costs on owners.

206884.explanation: Large solar farms are expensive and inefficient, place extra strain on the power grid, and take up valuable land that could be better used for housing or local industry.

206885.explanation: Sport is important, but subsidising memberships is not an essential municipal service. These schemes also have arbitrary cutoffs, disadvantaging people who fall just outside the threshold.

206886.explanation: Neighbourhood libraries are valuable, provided the locals actually make use of them. Mandating libraries in every neighbourhood is unnecessarily costly, and makes no sense if there is no demand. Case-by-case assessment is a more logical approach.

206887.explanation: Libraries are important and must remain accessible, but making it free for everybody is very costly, at the expense of other services.

206888.explanation: School buildings could be very useful to clubs, associations and initiatives, but only if there is a local need. We are not in favour of mandatory access, but do support specific local solutions, where schools only open their doors where practicable and there is a need.

206889.explanation: Municipal monuments tell Eindhoven’s history, and give our city character and identity. Investing in them will preserve them for future generations, instead of allowing them to vanish or fall into disrepair.

206890.explanation: Neighbourhood safety demands familiar faces with genuine authority, which is why this is first and foremost a job for the police and neighbourhood police officers. An additional BOA per neighbourhood will not solve the problem; instead it will create more fragmented enforcement.

206891.explanation: Safety on the streets is first and foremost a police concern, not one for municipal enforcement. BOAs are useful for monitoring and public order, but cannot replace the police by being deployed 24 hours a day.

206892.explanation: Enforcement officers should have a single clear presentation, making it clear to everybody that they act on behalf of the government. Religious symbols in uniforms create division, and do not belong as part of neutral street enforcement.

206893.explanation: BOAs are there to provide supervision and order, not to take on police duties. One way to attain greater safety is by putting more police on the streets, not by arming municipal enforcement.

206894.explanation: Safety on the streets is important, but more security cameras are not the solution. Cameras can be useful in specific problem hotspots, but should not lead to permanent surveillance and invasion of privacy.

206896.explanation: A zero-percent first-time-buyer loan will drive up housing prices and primarily help vendors, not starters. The solution lies in more affordable homes and lower taxes, not new loans that increase the debt burden.

206897.explanation: Moving house is a personal choice, not a municipal responsibility. This money could be better spent on lowering municipal taxes for everyone.

206898.explanation: Starters and young families have been on the bottom rung in the housing market for years, and need affordable houses now. Hard age quotas limit freedom of construction, and only make housing shortages worse.

206899.explanation: Eindhoven residents often wait years for an affordable home, and therefore deserve priority. This will make staying in the city feasible for people who have already built their lives here.

206900.explanation: Young people have been on waiting lists for years, and are being forced out of their own city. Reserving a percentage of social rentals for young people will once again give them a fair opportunity.

206901.explanation: Eindhoven residents have already been waiting too long for affordable homes. Construction should be prioritised, with due regard for the thoughtful integration of green spaces in new developments. Without any additional space for new housing, first-time buyers and families will be left behind, while the housing crisis only worsens.

206903.explanation: Welfare benefits are intended for people in real need, not to be abused. Cracking down on fraud will keep the system fair, ensuring that more remains for people who genuinely deserve support.

206904.explanation: Writing off debts does not address the root causes, and rewards irresponsible decisions, with hardworking Eindhoven residents footing the bill. Aid should focus on providing support and opportunities, not writing off debts with public funds.

206907.explanation: Free public transport sounds all well and good, but hardworking Eindhoven residents ultimately foot the bill through higher taxation.  Public transport should be made affordable for everyone via lower prices, instead of selective free schemes.

206908.explanation: For many people – including those with disabilities – cars are a necessity, not a luxury. More parking spaces enable them to travel in the city independently and without additional barriers.

206910.explanation: A low-traffic inner city will make Eindhoven less accessible for residents and businesses who are dependent on their car or van. Retail and hospitality will lose customers, and the city will become needlessly expensive and impractical for people who simply want to live, work, and shop.

206911.explanation: Creating a tram line is very costly and will disrupt the city for years. It would be better to invest in improved and affordable public transport.

206912.explanation: The planned reduction to 50km/h is an unnecessary punishment for drivers, and will mostly create frustration. A better investment would be in improving intersections, and smart solutions to allow traffic to flow smoothly and safely.

206913.explanation: A blanket 30km/h norm will lead to poor neighbourhood accessibility, and mainly increase frustration. Traffic safety is achieved through tailored solutions and thoughtful design, not by imposing an ideological speed-limit regime on the entire city.

206914.explanation: Air conditioning can help combat heat, but subsidies are not the right answer. Municipal funds should not go towards individual purchases, but instead contribute to lower taxes and solutions that benefit everybody.

206915.explanation: Informal carers do essential work for family and friends and deserve support, not extra costs. Free parking lowers barriers, and keeps care human-centred and close by.

206856.explanation: Economic growth is important in order to offer employment and opportunities to Eindhoven residents. New businesses are welcome, provided they contribute to fair work, proper employment conditions, and investments in training to ensure the whole city prospers.

206857.explanation: Collaboration with major employers can help to create jobs and innovation in the region. However, DENK believes it is essential for growth to go hand-in-hand with proper housing, fair wages and opportunities for local residents and young people.

206858.explanation: DENK favours healthy, safe neighbourhoods. We are committed to energy conservation, insulation, solar panels, and clean mobility, as these solutions are affordable, locally implementable, and give immediate benefits to residents.

206859.explanation: We believe that seniors should have an active voice in policy. With a seniors’ council, neighbourhood service centres, and strong support for informal carers, we can ensure their ideas directly impact municipal decisions.

206860.explanation: We believe that residents should have an active voice in policy. With citizens’ assemblies, resident panels, and experts with practical experience, we can ensure that decisions are fairer and better address local needs throughout Eindhoven.

206861.explanation: We prefer different modes of participation, such as resident panels, neighbourhood meetings, and the active engagement of experts with experience. This allows residents not only to give their opinions, but to make a genuine contribution, influencing topics and considerations prior to decision-making.

206862.explanation: We believe that residents should have a say in what happens in their neighbourhoods. Fixed neighbourhood budgets give residents the opportunity to realise vegetation, play areas, safety, and meeting places, making every neighbourhood fairer and more liveable.

206863.explanation: We believe that people on low and minimum wages should retain some financial flexibility. To prevent housing and living expenses from becoming unaffordable for residents on the tightest budgets, the OZB should therefore not rise any faster than inflation in the Netherlands.

206864.explanation: Sustainability is important to us, but everybody should be able to participate in the economy. We want to encourage and support businesses in their sustainability journey, instead of rejecting them out of hand, to ensure that jobs and opportunities continue to grow in Eindhoven.

206865.explanation: Public funds should go first to jobs, education, and support for local and social enterprise. Although promotion can help, our priority lies in creating strong neighbourhoods and fair opportunities for Eindhoven residents, so that economic growth benefits the city directly.

206866.explanation: We give priority to local businesses, jobs, and education for Eindhoven residents. Bringing back head offices is less important than ensuring that economic growth directly contributes to robust neighbourhoods and fair opportunities in the city.

206867.explanation: We believe that taxes should be fair to residents. Eliminating the dog tax will give people on minimum and low incomes more financial flexibility, and ensure that pet ownership does not become unnecessarily costly.

206868.explanation: We believe that local businesspeople should be free to decide for themselves what works best for their business and their neighbourhood. This way they can cater more efficiently to their customers and grow their business, without needless municipal regulation.

206869.explanation: Public spaces should contribute to a healthy and sustainable living environment – advertising for non-sustainable products does not belong there. We encourage conscious choices, and want the municipality to work proactively on a healthier future for everybody.

206870.explanation: We believe that shelter should be human-centred and small-scale. Spreading asylum seekers throughout the city reduces the local impact, improves support, facilitates contact with residents, and improves chances to participate in society.

206871.explanation: Recognised refugees are Eindhoven residents, and are entitled to a dignified start. Fast accommodation promotes integration, work, and education, and prevents long-term emergency shelter. We also wish to invest in more rental social housing, so that showing solidarity does not have to disadvantage others.

206872.explanation: We believe that newcomers should quickly become a part of the city. Additional investments in language, support, work, and neighbourhood activities will help them become independent more quickly, joining in with their local community and Eindhoven society.

206873.explanation: Eindhoven is stepping up and doing its part for the humane reception of asylum seekers. Additional numbers are possible, provided there is sufficient backing from the community and enough facilities and neighbourhood support, so that both newcomers and the current residents have what they need.

206874.explanation: We believe recognition is important for justice and unity. By acknowledging and apologising for our colonial and slavery past, we show that we are learning from it and building a future together based on respect and equal opportunity for all.

206875.explanation: We believe that public buildings should offer space for everyone. Silent rooms are spaces for contemplation and prayer, but also for people who need peace and quiet. This helps promote inclusion, respect, and well-being in our city.

206876.explanation: We believe that growth should be fair and responsible. New companies are welcome, but only if accommodation for labour migrants is in order. This will ease pressure on residential areas, and ensure proper accommodation for all.

206877.explanation: We believe a healthy living environment is important. While pollution does need to be addressed, we first need to help companies to become cleaner and more sustainable. Relocation is one possible option, where improvements are not possible and neighbourhoods experience ongoing impact.

206878.explanation: We believe that residents should be able to create greener, healthier neighbourhoods. With funding for shrubs and plants, residents can beautify their local areas together, improving air quality and creating more spaces to meet.

206879.explanation: We value healthy living environments, as well as recreation opportunities. Our focus lies on protecting nature and water quality and creating clear regulations, to ensure that people and planet remain in balance without blanket prohibitions.

206880.explanation: We believe a lively city is important, as well as safety and neighbourhood peace and quiet. Our focus lies on effective agreements with businesses, residents and local enforcement, to enable a vibrant nightlife without extra disturbance or risks.

206881.explanation: We believe that culture and social contact bring people together. Enabling festivals and parties broadens opportunities for local initiatives and diverse communities, strengthening connection, creativity, and creating a lively city for everybody.

206882.explanation: We believe that sustainability should be affordable for businesses. Municipal loans will help businesses to become greener, lower costs, and help create a healthy living environment without compromising jobs or the local economy.

206883.explanation: We believe that monuments should also be able to contribute to a healthy and sustainable city. More flexible regulations will enable sustainability improvements, such as insulation and energy efficiency measures, without sacrificing the building’s character.

206884.explanation: Land in Eindhoven is scarce, and essential for housing, nature, and liveable neighbourhoods. Sustainability efforts are better focused on installing solar panels on roofs and public buildings, so that we can generate clean energy without sacrificing space for residents.

206885.explanation: We believe that sports should be accessible to all. Municipal membership support will help people on low incomes to participate, stay healthy, and get to know people in their local clubs and neighbourhoods.

206886.explanation: We believe that opportunities for learning and community should be close by for everybody. A library in every neighbourhood will give residents greater access to reading, learning and digital assistance, and create a safe place for people to meet, develop, and find support.

206887.explanation: We believe that everybody has the right to access reading, learning, and digital assistance. Making libraries free will give young people, families, and those on low incomes more opportunities to develop and participate in society.

206888.explanation: We believe that school buildings should also contribute to the neighbourhood outside of school hours. Opening them up to associations and social organisations will strengthen community, sport, culture, and equal opportunities in all neighbourhoods.

206889.explanation: We believe that Eindhoven’s heritage is important to identity and unity. Additional funding for protection and maintenance will ensure that monuments are preserved and remain accessible to residents and for neighbourhood activities.

206890.explanation: We believe that safety starts with trust in the neighbourhood. Permanent neighbourhood BOAs will be more familiar with local residents and their problems, allowing more timely assistance, preventive action, and creating a safer and more pleasant living environment.

206891.explanation: We believe that safety is important at every moment of the day. 24/7 deployment of BOAs will create visible presence and enable rapid response, even during the week. This will increase the sense of safety in neighbourhoods, and allow disturbances and other problems to be addressed more quickly.

206892.explanation: We believe that uniforms are what make BOAs professional and recognisable. The wearing of a headscarf, kippah or turban does nothing to change that, but instead demonstrates that law enforcement reflects the diversity of Eindhoven and helps ensure that everyone feels welcome.

206893.explanation: Safety begins with trust and proximity. BOAs must be visible, approachable, and have a de-escalating presence. Working together with residents and focusing on neighbourhood-based prevention will improve safety without resorting to harsher measures.

206894.explanation: Security cameras can help to increase safety in locations with frequent disturbance. However, surveillance should always occur subject to clear privacy regulations and in combination with neighbourhood BOAs and prevention, to ensure that it remains human-centred.

206896.explanation: We believe that young people and first-time buyers should have more opportunities to acquire their own home. An interest-free loan can help those with limited savings to buy a home, and ensure that housing remains accessible to Eindhoven residents.

206897.explanation: We believe that seniors who downsize should be supported. With a relocation grant, we help people make a smooth transition, freeing up larger homes for families and first-time buyers and improving housing mobility in the city.

206898.explanation: Of all new homes built in Eindhoven, at least one-third should be suitable for people aged 50 or over.

206899.explanation: We believe that Eindhoven residents – including young people, first-time buyers, families, and seniors – should have fair access to a home in their own city. Priority for locals means that people who live, work, or provide care here are not left behind.

206900.explanation: We believe that new developments should follow the 40-40-20 rule: a large number of affordable homes, options for mid-range incomes, and a smaller portion for the upper segment. Within this distribution, young people, first-time buyers, and seniors should have a fixed allocation.

206901.explanation: We believe that expansion is sometimes necessary to ensure affordable housing. All the greenery that can be retained, should be. We will build with care and ensure new parks and green wedges, so that neighbourhoods remain healthy and liveable for everybody.

206903.explanation: We believe that more funding to combat welfare fraud is not the right approach. We do support fair and transparent monitoring without discrimination. Our priority lies in support, trust, and help with employment and debt, so that problems can be avoided.

206904.explanation: We believe that people in debt need opportunities for a new start. Even with smaller debts, support from the municipality can help create financial stability, providing guidance toward a steady income and more control over spending.

206907.explanation: We believe that people on a budget should enjoy good mobility. Free public transport helps them get to work, school, healthcare, and family, stopping poverty from leading to isolation and giving everybody equal opportunities in the city.

206908.explanation: We believe that accessibility means more than just more parking spaces. Our focus is on inclusive public spaces, reliable public transport, and safe sidewalks and buildings, so that people with disabilities can navigate the city independently.

206910.explanation: We want a liveable and accessible inner city. While public transport and cycling should be encouraged, cars should not become victims of policy. Many residents depend on their car for work, healthcare, and accessibility.

206911.explanation: Reliable public transport is important, but big, expensive projects must not impact affordability. We would like to focus on fast, accessible connections in all neighbourhoods, such as community buses and improvements to existing transport lines.

206912.explanation: The speed limit on the Eindhoven Rondweg should remain at 70 km/h.

206913.explanation: Road safety is of paramount importance, especially around schools and busy residential streets. Instead of a blanket regulation, we advocate for a discretionary approach to ensure accessibility for residents, businesses, and emergency services.

206914.explanation: Older residents should be protected from heat and health risks. Targeted cooling subsidies can help the people who need it most, such as the especially vulnerable or those who live in poorly insulated homes.

206915.explanation: People should be eligible for free parking at locations where they care for family or loved ones in Eindhoven.

206856.explanation: Brainport and ASML should be allowed to grow, but only if amenities such as housing, infrastructure, and liveability can keep pace.

206857.explanation: All Eindhoven residents should benefit from these economic successes.

206858.explanation: We should build a small modular reactor (SMR) to stabilise the power grid, and potentially combine it with the heating network.

206859.explanation: In addition to a youth council (jong040) and a social domain client council, a senior’s council is also needed.

206860.explanation: Citizen input via motions, initiatives and the ‘right to challenge.’

206861.explanation: Participation and consultation: don't talk about residents – ask them.

206862.explanation: Current local and neighbourhood support should be continued and improved.

206863.explanation: Municipal taxes should only be corrected for inflation.

206864.explanation: As a party, we support businesses large and small. 50PLUS is in favour of sustainability.

206867.explanation: Because the revenue goes into the general fund.

206868.explanation: As a party, we support businesses large and small. We are in favour of national guidelines for opening hours.

206869.explanation: It is not clear which products are sustainable.

206870.explanation: Eindhoven should keep to the same support criteria as other municipalities, to prevent excessive influx. Opportunists are not welcome.

206871.explanation: Equal opportunities for housing allocation! No preferential treatment for recognised refugees.

206872.explanation: We wish to guard against unjustified expenses, so that future generations are not saddled with large debts.

206873.explanation: Eindhoven should keep to the same support criteria as other municipalities, to prevent excessive influx. Opportunists are not welcome.

206874.explanation: Government authorities and the royal household have stepped up and made their apologies.

206875.explanation: There are currently enough facilities available.

206876.explanation: The same applies to current businesses, and temp agencies.

206877.explanation: Addressing high-polluting industry is on our policy platform. With economic power comes responsibilities. Economic growth must be accompanied by a pleasant living environment and sustainability.

206878.explanation: We should promote and encourage a green Eindhoven.

206879.explanation: Sport and culture are also important for a healthy and connected society.

206880.explanation: The recently adopted policy was developed with extensive input from residents and local businesses.

206881.explanation: The recently adopted policy was developed with extensive input from residents and local businesses.

206882.explanation: We advocate for a safe and healthy living environment, and policy that helps residents and businesses to take sustainability steps now and in the future.

206883.explanation: Sustainability must also be possible for city monuments.

206884.explanation: No wind or solar farms on scarce land.

206885.explanation: Sport must be affordable and accessible to all.

206886.explanation: Libraries for every neighbourhood are a part of our policy platform. 50PLUS wants strong communities and quality, accessible facilities. Libraries help create strong communities, and act as a meeting hub for everybody.

206887.explanation: We encourage social connectedness and fair treatment in our communities.

206888.explanation: Optimise the use of buildings by giving them a community role both day and night.

206889.explanation: Eindhoven must take good care of our monuments and protect them.

206890.explanation: More police presence on the streets, and safety in the neighbourhood.

206891.explanation: Safety, both day-to-day and online, should be a given for everybody.

206893.explanation: Enforcement officers (BOAs) should receive more powers and better equipment.

206894.explanation: More security cameras, including the use of sound recording, with consideration for privacy.

206896.explanation: 50PLUS supports a first-time-buyer loan, provided it is financially feasible.

206897.explanation: Building for the elderly to encourage mobility is the solution for the young.

206898.explanation: 50PLUS believes at least one-third of new housing developments should be suitable for those aged 50 or over.

206899.explanation: Local mobility and affordable housing is important for young and old.

206900.explanation: Suitable, affordable, and liveable homes for all.

206901.explanation: Eindhoven should be able to grow, but not at the expense of nature.

206903.explanation: Address all forms of fraud, including welfare fraud.

206904.explanation: This will vary, depending on the situation and the cause. Nobody should fall through the cracks.

206907.explanation: Free public transport should also fall under low-income support schemes in future.

206908.explanation: Add disabled parking spaces in more locations.

206910.explanation: We want a city centre that is safe and accessible to all. Cars remain an essential means of transport.

206911.explanation: The focus should be on improving bus lines and capacity.

206912.explanation: Keep current speed limits to ensure smooth traffic flow and prevent inner-city cut-through traffic.

206913.explanation: In neighbourhoods where everyone can move around safely and comfortably.

206914.explanation: A €2,000 air-conditioning subsidy for people aged 75 and over with heart, lung, or other chronic conditions.

206915.explanation: Parking in Eindhoven should always be free for all carers, informal or otherwise.

206856.explanation: Eindhoven already has a reputation as a highly innovative city with lots of tech startups. We would like to keep things that way, and attract new companies to Eindhoven so they can drive innovation here.

206857.explanation: The growth of ASML must not jeopardise the city’s identity. English is increasingly becoming the norm. Residents are sensing the pressure on the housing market due to the purchase of homes by investors. We must ensure that all Eindhoven residents benefit from the growth.

206858.explanation: Nuclear power is an indispensable component of a reliable, CO2-free energy network. The municipality must provide a clear direction in the energy transition, in the interests of residents and businesses.

206859.explanation: We believe an additional consultative body for this group is not necessary. Roughly half of eligible voters are aged over 50, and this is also the group most likely to turn out to elections.

206860.explanation: We do not believe that a citizens’ assembly is a productive form of public consultation, as there is a very high barrier to participation. Recommendations that are not adopted can also lead to frustration among residents who have been given false hope. We therefore favour referendums.

206861.explanation: Referendums force politicians to stand by their decisions and present them clearly to the public. For every major municipal decision, we therefore wish to hold a proactive referendum to give citizens input on important matters at an early stage.

206862.explanation: Local residents know the needs of their area better than public officials. We also believe in shared responsibility: the municipality must be able to trust its residents, and empower them to help contribute to a better city. This will ensure that neighbourhoods retain their unique character.

206863.explanation: We are committed to financial stability for residents. We do not want to burden them with unnecessary limitations; the municipality should therefore make sure the OZB does not rise faster than inflation, keeping Eindhoven liveable and affordable.

206864.explanation: We object to additional stifling regulations for businesses. Businesses need to be able to keep doing business without oppressive legislation. NSC Eindhoven is not against sustainability policy in and of itself, but wants to prevent businesses falling between the cracks because of it.

206865.explanation: In our view, this is not necessary. Eindhoven already has a reputation as a highly innovative city, with a host of established startups and SMEs.

206866.explanation: We support bringing businesses such as Philips to Eindhoven. However, we are wary of the potential consequences of doing so. We do not want large companies to be calling the shots in Eindhoven.

206867.explanation: Revenue from the dog tax often lands in the general fund, rather than being used specifically for dog facilities. It is also unfair given that there is no tax for owing other pets, such as cats, that can also cause nuisance or disturbance.

206868.explanation: Where possible, local businesses should be able to set their own opening hours, giving them as much freedom as possible to conduct business.

206869.explanation: We are sceptical of overbearing regulations. People are sensible enough to interpret advertising for themselves. The term ‘sustainability’ is also extremely broad, allowing anything that is not completely organic to be banned from advertising.

206870.explanation: We support shared responsibility. Often neighbourhoods are selected that are not expected to offer much resistance. We want every neighbourhood to step up and take responsibility. This will also ensure that the wealthier neighbourhoods with plenty of resources do their part for the city.

206871.explanation: Anybody in an untenable situation deserves the right to priority. However, our city’s housing crisis means we need to reduce the pressure on available housing.

206872.explanation: To maintain Eindhoven’s identity, we must fully commit to the integration of newcomers, via a comprehensive approach involving the residents of Eindhoven. We want to establish local networks that allow newcomers to integrate informally into their neighbourhoods.

206873.explanation: We are glad that Eindhoven is stepping up and receiving asylum seekers. However, we wish to restrict numbers to the legal requirement, so that other municipalities will be motivated to do the same. This will also help maintain public support for local accommodations.

206874.explanation: Given that the King has apologised for the Netherlands’ slave history, we see no additional need for Eindhoven to do so again.

206875.explanation: We greatly value the separation of church and state, and therefore wish to keep religious expression separate from government buildings.

206876.explanation: NSC is well-known for its role in combating labour-migration abuses. Everybody deserves a house that is also a home, which means it must also be a safe and pleasant environment. Employers who do not respect this should be kept out of the municipality.

206877.explanation: We are committed to a liveable city. It is therefore necessary for polluting companies to move to locations where they cause the fewest problems for residents.

206878.explanation: In our view, no additional subsidy is necessary. Through citizen initiatives, residents can take action and plant seeds themselves. This will also help conserve public funds.

206879.explanation: There is too little recreational fishing in municipal waters for it to be a major problem. We do not see the need to implement a ban.

206880.explanation: Absolutely! NSC believes that the current range of nightlife activities is far too limited. We want to make sure there is enough variety, especially for young people.

206881.explanation: We want the regulations to be as simple as possible, and to ensure that events only need to submit a full, new application the first time. This will keep paperwork manageable and the city attractive for events.

206882.explanation: Renters and homeowners can already apply for sustainability loans. We would like to remove the burden for businesses, and create a similar process  for those wanting to become more sustainable.

206883.explanation: We support accessible information from the municipality, with regulations that are as simple as possible without compromising quality.

206884.explanation: Eindhoven is a very high-density city. We therefore do not see the value in large solar farms, given that there are other alternatives, such as small modular reactors.

206885.explanation: This already exists, as the participation allowance (Meedoenbijdrage). In our view, this option should be promoted more widely. Everybody, regardless of their budget, should be able to lead a healthy life.

206886.explanation: Absolutely. We support even distribution across the city, to avoid having to travel to the city centre all the time. We also want residents to manage the libraries themselves, through a volunteer system facilitated by the municipality.

206887.explanation: Membership should be free for people on low incomes, but not for everybody:  memberships are already inexpensive, and it will only become more difficult for libraries to stay open without additional income.

206888.explanation: Absolutely. We want to give Eindhoven’s clubs and community organisations as many  opportunities as possible. So this should be facilitated where possible.

206889.explanation: Though we care deeply for our municipal monuments, we currently do not see the need to allocate additional funds for protection. We want to spend public funds thoughtfully, and do not see this as efficient.

206890.explanation: All Eindhoven neighbourhoods already have a neighbourhood agent. We support broadening this role, and believe these agents should build up local networks of trust in the neighbourhood. BOAs can offer support, but the neighbourhood police officer should remain the key figure.

206891.explanation: Absolutely. Too many Eindhoven residents, including women and children, feel unsafe out of doors. It is therefore important to make sure the entire city is safe.

206892.explanation: The strength of our police and enforcement officers lies in their role as neutral outsiders in situations that are sometimes emotionally charged. We therefore wish to strictly maintain this neutrality, by not allowing agents to display any religious symbols.

206893.explanation: BOAs are there to support the police force, and when the situation calls for it, police officers should act accordingly.

206894.explanation: Most cameras are currently still situated in the city centre. We also want residents in other parts of the city to feel safe. It is therefore important to place cameras at busy locations in the rest of the city, such as shopping centres, bus stops, and busy roads.

206896.explanation: As a municipality, we aim to spend tax revenue wisely. In our view, loans of this type should come from private lenders. We will also accelerate housing development by scrapping unnecessary regulations, and maintain the affordability standard to ensure that cheaper homes are built.

206897.explanation: Currently seniors already receive priority for rental social housing in the seniors segment. Senior citizens are also relatively wealthy in general, making it unfair for taxpayers to offer a relocation grant to all senior citizens.

206898.explanation: We are committed to the accessibility of the city, and that also means housing. Currently, however, seniors already receive priority for rental social housing in the seniors segment. One-third of all new homes is also quite a lot to reserve exclusively for seniors.

206899.explanation: At NSC Eindhoven, this is one of our key action points. Eindhoven’s growth is putting enormous pressure on the housing market. We want to ensure that people born and raised in Eindhoven can remain in the city, and are not forced away. Eindhoven must be a home as well as a city.

206900.explanation: To give young people a fair chance at their own home, NSC strongly supports this measure. Young people are currently struggling the most under the pressure on the social housing market. Too little rental social housing is being built, not to mention that waiting times are often eight years at least.

206901.explanation: The housing shortage is currently extreme, and we must make fair choices. Building homes must take precedence. However, we would prefer to build new homes with green spaces, so that residents do not just see concrete, but also have access to some fresh air.

206903.explanation: The NSC knows like no other what a harsh approach can do. We want to combat fraud, but focusing too much on this aspect will lead to a second ‘child-benefits scandal.’ Welfare fraud is also relatively rare, and focusing on it would be unproductive.

206904.explanation: The Netherlands has a debt industry that is not interested in helping people with debts, but just wants to make money. As municipalities we should therefore not write debts off, but take them on in order to work with the debtor on a human-centred solution to their problem.

206907.explanation: Free public transport in Eindhoven is a priority of NSC Eindhoven. The bus is an important part of people’s lives in Eindhoven, especially for those on low incomes. Our goal is to found the Eindhoven Transport Authority (Eindhovens Vervoersbedrijf, EVB), a transport company that will work to ensure affordable and high-quality public transport.

206908.explanation: NSC Eindhoven wants a city where nobody is left behind. That is why we are committed to making the city as accessible as possible, especially for those with disabilities.

206910.explanation: We believe the inner city should remain accessible, including for drivers. We therefore oppose the municipality’s traffic circulation plan, which aims to keep cars out of the city as much as possible.

206911.explanation: We are open to the idea of trams in Eindhoven. However, our priority is to improve the quality and affordability of existing public transport. We are therefore committed to founding the Eindhoven Transport Authority (Eindhovens Vervoersbedrijf, EVB), which will begin with bus lines, but may continue with tram lines in the future.

206912.explanation: NSC Eindhoven wants to give drivers enough freedom to get around, while also keeping the city accessible and functional. Reducing speed will create unnecessary bottlenecks. We therefore oppose the municipality’s traffic circulation plan, which aims to keep cars out of the city.

206913.explanation: To keep our neighbourhoods safe, NSC Eindhoven is open to reducing speed limits to 30km/h where this is not already the case.

206914.explanation: Where there is a proven need for air conditioning due to health reasons, we believe the municipality should provide support by means of a subsidy. Where this is not the case, it is completely up to the owner.

206915.explanation: Absolutely! We aim to ensure that informal carers in Eindhoven receive enough support.

206857.explanation: Not just high-tech. Eindhoven must collaborate in any case.

206859.explanation: Elderly people can already have a say. We have active seniors on the list who are not victims.

206861.explanation: The people must be heard. From the streets to the council.

206863.explanation: Own experience now shows that the property tax increased by as much as 22%. We find this disproportionate.

206865.explanation: That requires infrastructure and appropriate nightlife. Attracting companies is not necessary.

206868.explanation: Within the norms and frameworks, free entrepreneurship must be possible. Protect the small entrepreneur. Maximum opening hours per day.

206871.explanation: Definitely not! For years it has been difficult for Eindhoven’s youth and people in service jobs to find housing.

206874.explanation: Eindhoven must look further. Will we get an apology?

206876.explanation: Who will arrange that? Temporary workers from Eastern Europe are evicted from their housing upon dismissal. They become homeless, which must be prevented.

206882.explanation: We no longer have a municipal bank.

206883.explanation: The monument must not be damaged in any case in 'demolition yards'.

206884.explanation: Solar panels can be placed on the roofs of high-rise buildings.

206885.explanation: Certainly must pay extra for children.

206889.explanation: Not just more money, but truly more protection.

206890.explanation: We do have community police officers.

206892.explanation: Freedom of religion independent in public functions.

206893.explanation: We agree with that, however we believe that the BOAs should enforce serious matters and not on a chair leg that is 10 cm too far on a terrace.

206896.explanation: Is this a realistic question for a municipal bank?

206897.explanation: Elderly want to live in smaller homes but this is even made impossible.

206899.explanation: Priority for workers in care, aid, and education professions.

206900.explanation: Not by age but by individual urgency.

206901.explanation: Eindhoven has gone too far in matters of animals and environment. People are apparently less important.

206904.explanation: Depends on the reason they got in there.

206907.explanation: Everyone should be able to do that.

206908.explanation: More, more, more is nonsense. Only if really necessary and tackle abuse.

206910.explanation: It appears this is undesirable for SMEs and the majority of residents.

206912.explanation: In any case not lower. Definitely not 30.

206913.explanation: Not in all neighborhoods and areas. Can be divided.

206914.explanation: Why 75? Individual situation regarding heat stress.

206915.explanation: There must be a parking space.

206856.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees, as innovative tech companies contribute to employment and economic development in the Brainport Eindhoven region. This growth must remain in balance with liveability, accessibility and the interests of residents, so that development enhances the city as a whole.

206857.explanation: In line with our platform, PRO Eindhoven supports collaboration with high-tech companies, provided that growth is accompanied by investments in affordable housing, mobility, and resident services.

206858.explanation: PRO Eindhoven is committed to pursuing development through locally practicable and sustainable solutions. Nuclear power is at odds with municipal responsibility, and will not contribute to rapid improvements in liveability.

206859.explanation: This is in line with PRO Eindhoven’s core participation principle. Residents, including seniors, should be involved from the outset in policy that affects them.

206860.explanation: Citizens’ assemblies align with our commitment to permanent citizen panels and citizen checks on major projects. PRO Eindhoven also calls for a dedicated executive member for Citizen Participation, ensuring that resident involvement is systematic and that their input genuinely influences decisions.

206861.explanation: PRO Eindhoven supports referendums that actively involve residents at an early stage, and that serve as the democratic keystone of an open and well-considered participation process.

206862.explanation: Neighbourhood budgets are a key element of our platform. Nobody knows neighbourhoods like their residents, and they should be able to decide on improvements themselves.

206863.explanation: Responsible government means predictable costs for residents and businesses, as PRO Eindhoven emphasises in its platform.

206864.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wishes to set sustainability as a criterion for new businesses. Companies wanting to settle here must be sustainable or clearly on the path to sustainability, backed by clear agreements and municipal support.

206865.explanation: Cities become attractive through good governance, not advertising campaigns. Effective policy and a reliable government that cooperates with businesses are more important than promotional funding.

206866.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wants a level playing field for all businesses, not symbolic policy without any direct benefit for residents.

206867.explanation: This is an outdated tax without any immediate returns for the people; the revenue disappears into the general fund, while dog owners have additional administrative costs. PRO Eindhoven wants less bureaucracy and fair regulations that work for residents. This tax should therefore go.

206868.explanation: Businesses and residents themselves know what works. A local budget is in line with neighbourhood-centred and practical policy.

206869.explanation: PRO Eindhoven disagrees, because a general ban on advertising for non-sustainable products would be difficult to implement and enforce, and sustainability is better promoted through clear, targeted measures that work in practice.

206870.explanation: PRO Eindhoven’s election platform explicitly states that accommodation should be distributed fairly across the city, so as not to overburden vulnerable neighbourhoods.

206871.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wants fairness and equal opportunities in housing allocation.

206872.explanation: According to our platform, investments in language, work and social participation are essential for robust neighbourhoods and cohesion.

206873.explanation: Our platform prioritises community support, capacity, and liveability, while focusing on our statutory duties.

206874.explanation: Eindhoven should focus primarily on creating unity and moving forward. We value acknowledgement of, and education about, our colonial and slavery past, but prefer a thoughtful local approach that promotes cohesion and coexistence, rather than formal apologies.

206875.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wants inclusion and connection via tailored and practical solutions, but does not support a blanket requirement for quiet spaces in all public buildings.

206876.explanation: PRO Eindhoven is in favour of companies that contribute to employment and development, but does not see accommodation for labour migrants as a prerequisite that the municipality should set in advance.

206877.explanation: PRO Eindhoven disagrees, as not all polluting companies should automatically be forced to move. We favour a tailored approach: tackling nuisance and pollution through clear regulations, supervision, and sustainability measures, ensuring improvements in liveability and sustainability without unnecessary relocations.

206878.explanation: Investments in neighbourhoods and resident initiatives are a major policy thrust of PRO Eindhoven.

206879.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wants a balance between recreation, animal welfare, and clear regulations.

206880.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees, because a vibrant nightlife contributes to economic development and a lively city. Opening times could be extended, if done so with care and clear agreements on safety, surveillance, and liveability, and with due consultation of residents.

206881.explanation: Culture and events strengthen social cohesion and make for a lively city, as stated in our platform.

206882.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wants sustainable development by proactively supporting companies in their sustainability journey, to encourage progress instead of merely monitoring it.

206883.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wants to make listed buildings more energy-efficient and future-proof while preserving their historic value, and therefore supports clearer and more flexible regulations that allow for tailored solutions.

206884.explanation: PRO Eindhoven would prefer to use valuable land for housing and liveable neighbourhoods. Solar panels should therefore first be placed on roofs and existing buildings.

206885.explanation: This is one of our explicit platform items: equal opportunities for children and adults through sport.

206886.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wants all neighbourhoods to have access to library services, but prefers an affordable range of online and neighbourhood-centred, physical services.

206887.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees, as knowledge, information and development should be easily accessible to everybody. The library is already free for children; by making it free for all, we strengthen equal opportunities, education, and social participation for everyone.

206888.explanation: Making school buildings available outside school hours in a controlled and safe manner, with clear agreements in place, enables the more effective use of facilities and strengthens neighbourhoods and community organisations.

206889.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees, as the protection of municipal monuments contributes to a liveable and future-proof city with a strong identity.

206890.explanation: A local approach and familiar faces increase safety and trust.

206891.explanation: Security should be available and close by, as our platform states.

206892.explanation: These are already permitted in Eindhoven; PRO Eindhoven believes that BOAs should be neutral and professional, and that religious expression is no problem provided that safety, recognisability, and equal treatment are not compromised.

206893.explanation: PRO Eindhoven believes BOAs must be able to work safely, but supports the baton only if it clearly improves safety and subject to proper training, clear rules of engagement, and robust supervision.

206894.explanation: PRO Eindhoven supports permanent security cameras at busy and high-risk locations such as stations and nightlife areas, but not as a general blanket measure throughout the city.

206896.explanation: Supporting first-time buyers is a part of fair housing policy, provided it is financially responsible.

206897.explanation: PRO Eindhoven sees a relocation grant as costly and relatively ineffective. The impact is limited, and implementation is expensive. PRO Eindhoven therefore opts for long-term investments in homes for seniors, and improved mobility on the housing market.

206898.explanation: The city must be prepared for an ageing population and suitable housing.

206899.explanation: PRO Eindhoven believes it is reasonable for Eindhoven residents seeking housing to be prioritised as far as legally possible in the allocation of social housing, while preserving a fair and transparent system.

206900.explanation: Equal opportunities for young people are an explicit focus on our platform.

206901.explanation: PRO Eindhoven disagrees. New homes are necessary, but not at the expense of green surroundings. We favour thoughtful construction within the city and the judicious use of space, with retention of nature and liveability as important considerations in spatial decisions.

206903.explanation: PRO Eindhoven believes that the abuse of services should be addressed to ensure confidence in social security, with surveillance always going hand-in-hand with support and a human-centred approach.

206904.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees, as the purchase and cancellation of debts can give people facing serious financial difficulties a way forward, with initial support followed by supervision, aiming for a debt-free future.

206907.explanation: Mobility is necessary for participation in society.

206908.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees, as people with disabilities need parking spaces for accessibility and independence in their daily lives.

206910.explanation: PRO Eindhoven supports a low-traffic city centre where this improves liveability and safety, but believes the centre should remain easily accessible for residents, businesses, emergency services, and people with disabilities.

206911.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wants smart, affordable mobility solutions, not expensive, high-risk projects.

206912.explanation: This will improve traffic flow, road safety, and liveability.

206913.explanation: PRO Eindhoven supports a 30 km/h limit in residential areas where evidence shows it contributes to traffic safety and liveability, but believes that implementation should be tailored to each street and neighbourhood.

206914.explanation: PRO Eindhoven disagrees. A general air-conditioning subsidy is not the answer: we prefer a tailored approach and targeted (preferably sustainable) support for older people who truly need it.

206915.explanation: PRO Eindhoven believes that informal carers deserve practical support, so they can provide care without additional obstacles, such as parking expenses.

206856.explanation: ASML gives our region a unique position in the world. Strengthening that position by attracting new tech companies is a wise choice not only for our generation, but for those to come.

206857.explanation: We cannot pass up the opportunities offered by the continued development of ASML. ASML is the undisputed world leader, but continued investment is essential to retain and potentially extend that position.

206858.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes that nuclear power should be part of the energy mix that should be viewed not only as a part of the region, but more broadly as national policy. In our view, a few large nuclear power stations would be better than numerous small, regional ones.

206859.explanation: In our view, seniors are already adequately represented on the Eindhoven municipal council.

206860.explanation: We agree, but with a caveat: of course the council should listen to what is happening in society, just not necessarily via a rigid mechanism.

206861.explanation: The council has a mandate from Eindhoven residents, and with it the responsibility to govern the city and make balanced judgements. A referendum can be guided too easily by pure emotion.

206862.explanation: With the caveat that ‘annual’ and ‘fixed’ are formulated too rigidly for our liking. Neighbourhoods, perhaps organised into liveability teams, maintain close contact with the municipality’s area coordinators. That is where quality initiatives should be considered and discussed.

206863.explanation: This would be a lovely idea, but we are faced with major challenges that cost money. The ChristenUnie is aware that this can lead to higher costs for residents, the vast majority of whom also benefit from the increased strength of the region. We greatly applaud generous exemptions for households on low incomes.

206864.explanation: We believe this proposition is far too simplistic. Who decides when a business is ‘sustainable’? All companies should adhere to the permits issued, subject to strict municipal enforcement, and closure if necessary.

206865.explanation: We do not see this as a necessity. Eindhoven is already ‘on the map,' and companies are well aware of how to find us.

206866.explanation: Eindhoven should never have let the head office go in the first place, and trying to get it back now seems pointless, in our view.

206867.explanation: This is inevitable in the long term — it is the national trend. But the loss of revenue will ultimately have to be covered by people who do not own a dog. Compared to the total costs of owning a dog, the dog tax is a token amount.

206868.explanation: This proposition is far too broad; local businesses can already set their own opening and closing hours, but are often forced to stay open to compete with large national chains. We do see the dangers of the 24-hour economy, however, and the value of enough quiet time in society.

206869.explanation: When does a product count as ‘sustainable’? There are plenty of products that are banned from advertising (alcohol, tobacco). This policy should be regularly reviewed, and updated where necessary.

206870.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes it has a responsibility to offer asylum seekers proper housing and opportunities – though not without boundaries. This is a national issue requiring local action. We believe asylum seekers should start contributing to society as soon as possible, e.g. through schooling, employment, or volunteer work.

206871.explanation: The housing market is complex and constrained. We must act wisely, and tailored solutions are better than blanket measures. What we need most of all are lots – and lots – of new homes.

206872.explanation: ‘More money’ is rarely a solution. We must do what is right, and integration is certainly a part of that. Our priority is to teach newcomers the language as quickly as possible (English if necessary), and enable them to contribute to society through paid or volunteer work.

206873.explanation: Even if we only do our national part, we will still have more than enough problems to solve on the housing market.

206874.explanation: This is is not an issue for Eindhoven. This is a national issue.

206875.explanation: Peace and quiet is a scarce commodity nowadays. A silent room would be very welcome.

206876.explanation: Businesses have a responsibility to their employees.

206877.explanation: But we want to go a step further: De Hurk should be rezoned as residential in its entirety. All industry must disappear. An added advantage is that the houseboats in Beatrixkanaal can stay where they are.

206878.explanation: All worthwhile initiatives from neighbourhoods should be taken seriously and implemented, including planting, landscaping, and green spaces.

206879.explanation: For many, fishing is a calming and relaxing activity. We do not want to take this away.

206880.explanation: In our view, current regulations already offer plenty of options.

206881.explanation: In our view, current regulations already offer plenty of options.

206882.explanation: Businesses can simply present their investment plans to the bank or to their shareholders. We see no role for the municipality here.

206883.explanation: Monuments should ‘stay as they are’ as much as possible, which demands great caution when making modifications.

206884.explanation: We simply do not have the space. We do, however, have hundreds of thousands of square metres of roof space that is currently unoccupied.

206885.explanation: Sports should be accessible to everyone, and money must not be a limitation.

206886.explanation: Eindhoven should never have cut library funding. Libraries should return in their former numbers, at a minimum.

206887.explanation: Many residents can easily afford a membership, and those who cannot should receive support.

206888.explanation: Who will monitor this? Who will open and close the buildings? All schools should be free to decide whether they make their buildings available, and to whom. The municipality should not impose any obligations in this respect.

206889.explanation: Our municipal monuments are already few in number, but we should cherish those we have, even if that means spending more.

206890.explanation: And they should also have a visible presence and be approachable for everyone. They should not be stuck in meetings in a neighbourhood centre somewhere.

206891.explanation: The lack of enforcement is a major concern. Enforcement is always necessary, which means 24/7.

206892.explanation: The Netherlands has the constitutional right to freedom of religion. Enforcement officers also need to be able to wear Christian religious symbols. One condition, however, is that these symbols do not prevent them from doing their job.

206893.explanation: BOAs must be enabled to do perform their duties properly, which may even require more than a baton.

206894.explanation: Not more cameras per se, but the opportunity to deploy more. We believe more enforcement presence would be more effective.

206896.explanation: The municipality is not a bank. We must ensure housing development.

206897.explanation: Seniors are quite willing to move, and in many cases can pay for it themselves. It is the municipality’s responsibility to ensure a broad range of adequate housing. That will encourage mobility automatically.

206898.explanation: We need to build what is needed, which could be more or less than 33%. The important thing is to get building!

206899.explanation: We need to be extremely careful allocating priority, as someone else is always disadvantaged.

206900.explanation: We need to build what is needed, which could be more or less than 25%. The important thing is to get building!

206901.explanation: We must not paint ourselves into a corner. Green areas that may need to go can be compensated for elsewhere. We must constantly strive for a balance – but build we must.

206903.explanation: Not more money, enough is being spent already. Far greater social evils include undermining and drug crime, where there is far more to be gained for society.

206904.explanation: This would definitely send the wrong message. People with problematic debts should receive quality, long-term support, which requires a tailored approach.

206907.explanation: There are effective schemes available for people on low incomes. Let us keep those first, and adapt them where necessary.

206908.explanation: We believe there is enough disabled parking. New developments could require more, which will demand tailored solutions.

206910.explanation: We would prefer a completely car-free city, provided that parking garages remain accessible.

206911.explanation: A tram would add nothing whatsoever to public transport in Eindhoven. We have the high-quality public transport system (HOV) with dedicated bus lanes, which we should continue to expand.

206912.explanation: 70 km/h, with phased traffic lights as much as possible. Ultimately, all intersections on the ring road should be grade-separated.

206913.explanation: 30km/h should be introduced (and enforced) where necessary, but not everywhere.

206914.explanation: Many people can afford this quite easily. For those who need it but cannot afford it, we must look at tailored solutions.

206915.explanation: This sounds like a good idea, but is not enforceable and should be avoided.

206856.explanation: EVE agrees here. Tech companies create prosperity and the jobs of the future, and Eindhoven plays a key role in their progress. At the same time, new companies’ growth should be socially responsible, so that economic development does not compromise liveability, affordability, or quality of life.

206857.explanation: ASML is extremely valuable to the prosperity of the region and the future. At the same time, the municipality must not focus on the growth of a single company, but instead on a healthy economy and a prosperous society, where businesses can both thrive and contribute their fair share.

206858.explanation: Nuclear power is a complex national issue. Eindhoven is not the designated authority to decide on the matter.

206859.explanation:
EVE believes that citizen participation is essential. Both seniors and younger generations should be properly engaged in policymaking.
 We facilitate citizen input through a range of participatory mechanisms.
 Council members are selected at random, ensuring equal opportunity and accurate representation.

206860.explanation: EVE sees the citizens’ assembly as a key instrument for giving residents genuine influence over municipal policy. If carefully organised, it brings together a representative group of Eindhoven residents to work on broadly supported solutions, strengthening local democracy.

206861.explanation: EVE supports resident input, but does not see referendums as the right instrument. They only offer a yes/no choice without room for nuance, dialogue, or joint consideration, and increases the risk of polarisation and narrowing the debate.

206862.explanation: EVE supports the availability of neighbourhood budgets that residents can use to improve their local areas. These already exist, but we would like to give them more prominence. Proper guidance, clear information and fair support are necessary, so that all neighbourhoods can reap the benefits.

206863.explanation: As a rule, EVE believes that the OZB should not rise faster than inflation, to keep living costs manageable for the average citizen. At the same time, we also want more differentiation, so that wealthier households contribute more than those on lower incomes.

206864.explanation: EVE believes that sustainability is important, and expects companies to be committed to the climate and liveability. Strict admission requirements are not realistic, however. We prefer encouragement and realistic obligations with clear standards and enforcement, so that Eindhoven can improve sustainability without foregoing economic opportunities.

206865.explanation: Eindhoven is already appealing to businesses, thanks to robust city marketing. More funding for promotion should therefore not be the automatic option. EVE wants public funds to promote liveability, services, and space for residents. A resident-friendly city is also a business-friendly city.

206866.explanation: Philips and Eindhoven have a long history, and the return of the head office could increase employment, expertise, and prestige. We believe the municipality should be able to make efforts, subject to reasonable conditions and in balance with the city’s interests. Philips must also be willing, of course.

206867.explanation: EVE supports eliminating the dog tax. It is not a large source of income and mainly affects low-income earners, while dogs offer companionship to singles and seniors especially, helping to combat isolation.

206868.explanation: EVE believes it is important for businesses to do business, but complete freedom of opening hours creates unfair competition and puts additional strain on employees. Joint agreements ensure fair competition, proper working conditions, and liveable neighbourhoods.

206869.explanation: EVE values sustainability, but questions whether it is the municipality’s role to determine which products are morally acceptable through advertising bans. The government should set clear boundaries and encourage sustainable choices, while leaving the decision up to residents and businesses.

206870.explanation: EVE supports the humane and thoughtful accommodation of asylum seekers. Distribution across the city avoids excessive strain on neighbourhoods, increasing community support and integration. Small-scale accommodation with clear agreements and proper support are essential in this regard.

206871.explanation: EVE believes that recognised refugees have the right to proper accommodation and occasionally priority, but not across the board and not only for social rentals. We favour a balanced approach with more homes, so that nobody is left out and the housing shortage is solved instead of merely transplanted.
