206872.explanation: EVE believes that investments in the integration of newcomers is essential. Effective support for language, work, education, and community participation means they gain independence and can take part sooner, which is in the interests of both newcomers and the city.

206873.explanation: Accepting more than the legal requirement should not be a goal in itself. It depends on the circumstances at hand. It may increase in times of crisis; at other times the amount may vary. Quality, distribution, and solidarity are the important elements.

206874.explanation: What matters most is that the municipality, in close dialogue with descendants, continues to address the history of slavery and colonialism with recognition, acknowledgement of its ongoing effects, and unity as the goal. That will reveal whether this kind of step makes sense.

206875.explanation: EVE supports a clear separation of church and state. However, a silent space for anybody who needs it – including for non-religious purposes – could be a valuable addition.

206876.explanation: EVE believes that economic growth should never compromise liveability or space for housing. Labour migrants need protection against exploitation and poor accommodation. More controls are needed.

206877.explanation: De Hurk is situated beside residential areas. EVE considers persistent nuisance and health risks unacceptable, but solutions must be carefully designed to avoid merely shifting the problem, to support sustainability where possible, and to keep workers and liveability in mind.

206878.explanation: EVE believes that neighbourhood greenery improves liveability, health, and climate resilience. Financially supporting residents to add greenery to their neighbourhoods strengthens engagement. The municipality should be proactive, offering encouragement and proper support, so that all neighbourhoods can benefit.

206879.explanation: EVE wonders whether we should have an opinion here. For many residents, fishing is a relaxing activity.

206880.explanation: EVE supports a vibrant nightlife and sees possibilities for extended opening hours, but only under clear conditions and with due regard for liveability, safety, and evening rest. Tailored solutions, effective enforcement, and clear agreements are essential.

206881.explanation: EVE believes that festivals and parties contribute to liveliness and community in Eindhoven, and should be easier to organise. The municipality should support initiators more proactively with guidance, clear procedures, and suitable locations, with attention to safety and liveability.

206882.explanation: EVE feels municipal loans for sustainability should fall under a city development entity, affording the municipality an active role in investing in sustainability, housing, and employment. The loans should be purpose-driven and revolving, so that public funds yield returns locally.

206883.explanation: EVE believes that sustainability of monuments can be improved without loss of historic value. Rigid rules currently stand in the way. With smarter customisation, listed buildings can be made more energy-efficient and still be preserved for the future.

206884.explanation: EVE believes that scarce land in Eindhoven should first be used for housing and amenities, not large solar farms. We would rather put solar panels on roofs, façades, and industrial estates, so that the energy transition does not compromise liveability or the environment.

206885.explanation: EVE believes that everyone should have access to sport, regardless of their income. The municipality can broaden access by providing targeted contributions toward club memberships. This will create equal opportunities, improve health, and foster social connection.

206886.explanation: EVE believes city libraries are indispensable as accessible meeting places that offer far more than books alone. They are certainly important as part of liveable neighbourhoods. Every district already has one, but their number could be increased.

206887.explanation: EVE believes that library access should be free for all. Learning and culture should not be dependent on income. Free library access lowers barriers and contributes to education, participation, and equal opportunities, which justifies public spending.

206888.explanation: EVE believes that school buildings should also serve neighbourhood needs. Making them available to clubs and organisations outside school hours could be a more efficient use of space and promote social connections and community, provided it is done safely and effectively.

206889.explanation: EVE believes that municipal monuments are an important part of Eindhoven’s identity and history. Investing in protection and maintenance preserves this heritage, allowing it to be used actively in the city and local areas for the long term.

206890.explanation: EVE believes that safety and liveability start locally. Permanent neighbourhood BOAs foster familiarity and trust, bridge the gap to residents, and make tackling disturbance more effective. This way, enforcement becomes more human-centred and neighbourhood-focused.

206891.explanation: EVE wants community enforcement officers to be available where and when they are needed, to ensure safety and quality of life, including on weekdays and at night. This should not be interpreted as a 24/7 standard. Deployment should be targeted and proportional, coordinated with the police, and with due regard for working conditions.

206892.explanation: EVE believes that BOAs should have a neutral appearance. Religious symbols do not belong on public-service uniforms. This way, enforcement officers are recognisable and impartial to everyone, and can carry out their work with authority and in a way that brings people together.

206893.explanation: EVE believes that BOAs should be safe at work, but we do not know whether a baton is the solution. For this reason, we would like to conduct a pilot to ascertain the value of a baton.

206894.explanation: EVE greatly values safety. Cameras can be of help at specific locations, but should not be a blanket solution. Privacy and proportionality take precedence. Prevention, enforcement presence, and robust neighbourhoods remain essential, although video surveillance can increase public safety.

206896.explanation: EVE believes that first-time buyers with a low or medium income deserve support, but only selectively and in a supplementary manner. Generic loans drive up prices. The real solution lies in building enough affordable homes: the housing shortage needs to be solved, not transplanted.

206897.explanation: EVE wants to encourage mobility on the housing market. A relocation grant for seniors could help to free up larger homes for families. This grant should be targeted, temporary and issued through a municipal development company, provided there are enough suitable homes for older people.

206898.explanation: EVE believes that new developments should focus more on lifelong homes and mobility for seniors. They are the largest group in Eindhoven, after all.

206899.explanation: EVE believes that Eindhoven residents should have fair access to rental social housing in their own city. Giving priority to local residents when allocating housing helps strengthen their connection to the city and reduces local housing shortages, while following the law and protecting vulnerable groups.

206900.explanation: EVE believes that young people deserve their own space on the housing market, and that there are too few suitable homes for them. Social rentals service a large demographic, and are often too expensive for students. We are in favour of tailored solutions: dedicated homes and housing models for young people, alongside sufficient social rentals for everybody.

206901.explanation: EVE believes that the housing shortage is serious, and more construction is needed. This could occur in current green zones, subject to strict conditions and compensation elsewhere. Green spaces remain essential. That is why we plan to focus on multi-functional building inside the city, and using space wisely.

206903.explanation: EVE believes that the municipality should operate on the basis of trust, not distrust. Less monitoring gives welfare recipients more opportunities to move on to work or education, and gives officials more time to provide support. This is a more effective, efficient, and humane approach.

206904.explanation: EVE believes that welfare recipients with problem debts deserve opportunities. Targeted debt buy-back and partial debt relief give people breathing space to get back out into the community and re-enter work or education, with proper support.

206907.explanation: EVE believes that transport should not be a luxury. Even people on a budget should be able to get around. Free public transport prevents social exclusion, increases resilience, and helps people participate actively in society.

206908.explanation: EVE believes that Eindhoven should be accessible to all. Additional disabled parking spaces are necessary to promote independence. Investments in this area foster equal opportunities, improved accessibility, and an inclusive city.

206910.explanation: EVE believes that a low-traffic city centre fosters liveability and safety. Less car traffic means cleaner air and more space for walking and cycling, provided it is implemented thoughtfully, gradually, and with proper access for residents, businesses, and people with disabilities.

206911.explanation: EVE believes that Eindhoven should invest in more high-quality public transport. A tram would increase accessibility, reduce car traffic, and support sustainable growth. Investments now will avoid higher costs and bigger problems later.

206912.explanation: EVE believes that the speed limit on the Eindhoven ring road should remain at 70km/h. It strikes a good balance between traffic flow, safety, and liveability. Further speed reductions should only occur where clearly necessary, so that the ring road remains accessible.

206913.explanation: EVE believes that residential areas are for living in, and should not be treated as through-roads. A speed limit of 30km/h increases road safety, reduces noise pollution, and makes streets safer for children, seniors, and cyclists.

206914.explanation: EVE believes that protection against extreme heat is important, especially for vulnerable seniors. However, we do believe that financial support should go to those who want air conditioning but cannot afford it.

206915.explanation: EVE believes that informal carers are indispensable, and that they should be able to provide care without additional obstacles. Free parking on site can help, provided it is issued rigorously and on a case-by-case basis via an informal carer scheme, to prevent misuse.

shootoutStatements.title: Every Eindhoven district should receive a fixed annual amount that residents can use to improve their own neighbourhood.
Together, Eindhoven and Veldhoven should fully support the growth of high-tech company ASML.
The municipality should prohibit advertising for non-sustainable products in public spaces.
The Property Tax (OZB ) should not rise any faster than average prices in the Netherlands (inflation).
The municipality should permit construction of a small modular (nuclear) reactor.
Eindhoven should make apologies for its history of colonialism and slavery.
The municipality should invest more to promote Eindhoven as an attractive business location.
The municipality should set up the citizens’ assembly, which advises the municipal council. Citizens’ assembly members are chosen at random.
Asylum-seeker accommodation should be spread out across the city.
The municipality should only support the arrival of new businesses if there is proper accommodation for migrant workers.
The municipality should give money to residents who wish to plant more shrubs and plants in their neighbourhoods.
The sustainability regulations for historic monuments should be more lenient.
Of all new homes built in Eindhoven, at least one-third should be suitable for people aged 50 or over.
Companies wishing to become more sustainable should be eligible for a loan from the municipality.
The municipality should co-finance sports club memberships for people on low incomes.
The municipality should not make any land available for large solar farms.
The municipality should try to get the Philips head office back to Eindhoven.
The municipality should create more disabled parking spaces.
Every district in Eindhoven should have its own library.
Eindhoven should build a tram line.
School buildings should be open in the evenings and on weekends for use by clubs/associations and civil-social organisations.
Every Eindhoven district should have a permanent municipal enforcement officer (BOA).
Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) should be deployable 24 hours a day, including on weekdays.
At least 25% of all new social rental housing should go to young people.
People on low incomes should remain eligible for free public transport in Eindhoven.
